architecture_summary: MaxViT is a hierarchical vision backbone that stacks repeated MaxViT blocks, each combining MBConv with blocked local attention and dilated global grid attention. The multi-axis attention module is designed to provide both local and global spatial interactions with linear complexity and no explicit positional encoding.

novel_contributions: A new efficient and scalable attention model called multi-axis attention, consisting of blocked local and dilated global attention.
A new hierarchical vision backbone, dubbed MaxViT, by repeating the basic building block over multiple stages.
A stand-alone multi-axis attention module that can be used either standalone or combined in any order.
A simple modular design that unifies convolution, local attention, and global attention.
Strong results across image classification, object detection, image aesthetic assessment, and image generation.

key_claims.claim: MaxViT introduces multi-axis attention that decomposes full self-attention into blocked local attention and dilated global grid attention, giving linear complexity while preserving global-local interactions.
MaxViT is a hierarchical backbone built by stacking repeated MaxViT blocks that combine MBConv with block attention and grid attention.
The paper claims MaxViT achieves state-of-the-art performance on ImageNet-1K under various settings, including 86.5% without extra data and 88.7% with ImageNet-21K pre-training.
On ImageNet-1K, MaxViT-L sets a new performance record of 85.17% at 224 × 224 training without extra training strategies, and MaxViT-L reaches 86.70% at 512 × 512.
On ImageNet-21K pre-trained models, MaxViT-XL achieves 88.51% at 384 and 88.70% at 512, which the paper describes as new SOTA performance.
On COCO2017 detection and instance segmentation, MaxViT backbones outperform Swin, ConvNeXt, and UViT by large margins in both accuracy and efficiency.
On AVA, MaxViT-T reaches 0.745 PLCC and 0.708 SRCC at 512 × 512, and the paper states it outperforms existing image aesthetic assessment methods.
On ImageNet unconditional generation, MaxViT achieves 30.77 FID and 22.58 IS with 18.6M parameters, outperforming HiT in the reported table.
